docs.title: Documentation
docs.overviewIntro: ThreadMind is an open-source local web interface that organizes your AI-assisted development into hierarchical threads. Each thread maintains its own context, making conversations focused and efficient. It runs on your machine — your data stays local, your API keys stay private.
docs.featureThreadsDesc: Organize discussions hierarchically. Create sub-threads for sub-tasks, reference sibling threads for shared context.
docs.featureContextDesc: Automatic context optimization with summaries, pinned messages, and cross-thread references. Save tokens without losing context.
docs.featureMultiProviderDesc: Switch between Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, DeepSeek, Mistral, and Meta models. Use the best model for each task.
docs.featureTeamDesc: Share threads with your team via git. Imported threads are read-only and serve as context for your own work.
docs.gsInstallDesc: This starts the ThreadMind server locally on port 3000. No account needed.
docs.gsApiKeyDesc: You need at least one AI provider API key to start chatting.
docs.gsApiKeyStep1: Click the gear icon (top right) to open Settings
docs.gsApiKeyStep2: Enter your API key for any supported provider
docs.gsProjectDesc: A project links to a target directory on your machine. This is the codebase the AI will work with. Choose "Solo" for personal use or "Team" if you plan to share threads via git.
docs.gsFirstThreadDesc: Click "+" next to Threads to create your first root thread. Give it a descriptive title. Then type your message and hit Send. That's it — you're ready to develop with AI.
docs.conceptThreadsDesc: Threads are the core unit. Each thread is a focused conversation about a specific topic or task. You can nest sub-threads to break down complex work. Parent thread context (summaries + pinned messages) flows down to children automatically.
docs.conceptThreadsTip: Tip: Create a root thread per feature, then sub-threads for implementation details like "database schema", "API endpoints", "UI components".
docs.conceptContextDesc: The context engine assembles the optimal prompt for each message. It prioritizes information by type:
docs.conceptReferencesDesc: Reference other threads to pull in their context (summaries) without duplicating content. Useful when one thread needs awareness of decisions made in another.
docs.conceptPinningDesc: Pin important messages (decisions, architecture choices, key specs) to ensure they're always included in child thread contexts. Pinned messages persist across summary regeneration.
docs.providersIntro: ThreadMind supports 6 AI providers. Each uses your own API key — no proxy, no middleman. Enable multiple providers and switch between models per message.
docs.providersTip: You can enable multiple providers simultaneously and switch between models on each message using the model selector in the chat input area.
docs.modesIntro: Each message can be sent in one of two modes, controlling whether the AI can modify your project files.
docs.planFeature1: Conversation only — no file system access
docs.planFeature2: Ideal for brainstorming, planning, code review
docs.planFeature3: No tool calls, no approval prompts
docs.agentFeature1: AI can read files and directories
docs.agentFeature2: AI can write and edit files (with your permission)
docs.agentFeature3: Each write operation requires explicit approval
docs.agentFeature4: After approval, the AI continues automatically
docs.modesWarning: In Agent mode, write operations (create/edit files) always require your explicit approval. Read operations (list directory, read file) execute automatically.
docs.teamIntro: Team mode enables sharing AI conversation threads with your team via git. The database stays local (individual), but thread content is exported as markdown files in a .threadmind/ folder inside your project.
docs.teamStep1: Set your author name in Settings
docs.teamStep2: Export your threads — markdown files are generated in .threadmind/
docs.teamStep4: Teammates pull and import — your threads appear as read-only in their UI
docs.teamRule1: Your threads are read-write locally, read-only for others
docs.teamRule2: Imported threads serve as reference context only
docs.teamRule3: The SQLite database is never shared — only .threadmind/ markdown files
docs.shortcutPanelsDesc: Use the sidebar toggles and the doc/file explorer buttons on the right edge to show or hide panels.
